rows.snippet: Release Notes for WebLink 8.2.2. 12 years ago 1013209 https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1013209/release-notes-for-weblink-8-2-2- WebLink 8.2.2 Summary Laserfiche WebLink 8.2.2 is a maintenance release that includes ...... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1013209/release-notes-for-webli ...
rows.title: Release Notes for WebLink 8.2.2.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Sign in Release Notes for WebLink 8.2.2. Product: WebLink 8.2.2 KB: 1013209 Published: April 15, 2013 Last Updated: April 28, 2014 Summary Laserfiche WebLink 8.2.2 is a maintenance release that includes miscellaneous updates and hotfixes released for WebLink 8.2.1. For a more detailed listing of changes, please see Knowledge Base article 1013208. WebLink Server System Requirements Minimum requirements CPU: 2 GHz or faster processor Memory: 1 GB RAM Operating system: Windows XP (Service Pack 2) with IIS 5, Windows Server 2003 (Service Pack 1) with IIS 6, Windows Vista with IIS 7, Windows Server 2008 with IIS 7, Windows 7 with IIS 7.5, Windows Server 2008 R2 with IIS 7.5, Windows 8 with IIS 8, Windows Server 2012 with IIS 8 Recommended Requirements CPU: 2.8 GHz or faster processor Memory: 2 GB RAM Server operating system: Windows Server 2003 with IIS 6, Windows Server 2008 with IIS 7, Windows Server 2008 R2 with IIS 7.5, Windows Server 2012 with IIS 8 Note: Hardware requirements may fluctuate based on the number of users logged in to the server. If you expect to have many simultaneous connections to your Web Access server, we encourage you to configure it with a faster CPU and/or add more RAM. Compatibility Information Laserfiche Server 8: Laserfiche WebLink 8.2 requires version 8.2 or later of the Laserfiche Server. WebLink is not compatible with standalone editions of Laserfiche (Executive, Desktop, etc.). WebLink is an ASP.NET 2.0 Web application for IIS. If you are running IIS 7 or IIS 8, make sure the IIS Metabase and IIS 6 configuration compatibility (under IIS 6 Management Compatibility) feature is enabled for your IIS 7 installation. If you are using Windows Server 2008 R2, you must enable the .NET Framework 3.5.1. Features option from the Add Features Wizard in the Server Manager prior to installing WebLink. Client Workstation: WebLink is best viewed using Internet Explorer 6, Internet Explorer 7, Internet Explorer 8, Internet Explorer 9, Firefox 3 or later, Safari 4 or later, Chrome 10 or later, Opera 11. Note: Internet Explorer 6 and 7 users should install Microsoft security update 947864 (MS08-024). Mobile Device: WebLink has been optimized for the iPhone, Android, Blackberry Storm, Blackberry Curve, and Blackberry Torch. However it may be functional on other mobile browsers. General Information If you are using Windows Server 2008 R2, you must enable the .NET Framework 3.5.1 Features option from the Add Features Wizard in the Server Manager prior to installing WebLink. Laserfiche WebLink works with Microsoft's Internet Information Services (IIS) to provide access to the documents in the Laserfiche repository. WebLink requires Internet Information Services 5.1 or above. This section describes some of the additional configuration that may be necessary to run WebLink on IIS 6 or IIS 7. A default installation of IIS 6.0 only serves static content. Ensure IIS 6.0 is configured to allow ASP.NET content. The IIS metabase stores configuration information for your installation of IIS. Some settings can't be edited using the IIS manager MMC snap-in, however, IIS includes a feature that allows you to directly edit the metabase XML while IIS is running. Ensure you enabled this feature. Ensure the following features are installed for IIS 7 and IIS 8: IIS WMI compatibility IIS Metabase and IIS 6 configuration compatibility IIS Scripting Tools IIS Management Service ASP.NET Windows Authentication By default, the WebLink 8.2 document viewer will not display the folder breadcrumb above the image display. You can use the Show Breadcrumb option on the Document Viewer tab in the WebLink Designer to enable the control. WebLink 8.0.1 introduced language packs support. During the WebLink installation, select the Custom setup type to choose to manually install available Language Packs. By default, if you choose to display the installation in a non-English language, the installation attempts to automatically install the corresponding Language Pack. When logging in through WebLink, you must specify a user account configured for read-only access for WebLink to properly use retrieval licenses. Similarly, the Public Portal Licensing and Web Distribution Portal Licensing features only take effect when logged in with a user account configured for read-only access. Installation Information WebLink is available in both 32-bit and 64-bit. By default, the installation process will automatically install the version corresponding to your version of Microsoft Windows. Because IIS 6.0 does not support running 32-bit and 64-bit worker processes concurrently, you may need to explicitly install the 32-bit version of WebLink on a 64-bit version of Windows Server 2003 or Windows XP. The WebLink installation's setup.exe supports a /force32bit command-line parameter. Click Start, All Programs, Accessories, and click Command Prompt to load a command prompt. At the command prompt, type the following: "C:\temp\WebLink8.2\e

rows.title: Release Notes for Laserfiche Workflow 10.4.2
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith Release Notes for Laserfiche Workflow 10.4.2 Product: Workflow KB: 1014101 Published: December 18, 2019 Last Updated: November 2, 2020 Summary Laserfiche Workflow 10.4.2 includes various feature improvements and bug fixes. For a detailed list of changes, please see Knowledge Base article 1014089. System Requirements Supported Operating Systems Windows 7 SP1 Windows 8.1 Windows 10 Windows Server 2008 R2 SP1 Windows Server 2012 Windows Server 2012 R2 Windows Server 2016 Windows Server 2019 Note: 32-bit and 64-bit versions supported for all Workflow 10 and higher components. Note: If you are running Windows 8 or Windows Server 2012, you must install .NET Framework 4.5, 4.6, or 4.7 before installing Workflow. Requirements for the Workflow Designer Minimum: CPU 1GHz, 512MB RAM. Recommended: CPU 2.4GHz, 1GB RAM or higher. Requirements for the Workflow Server and Subscriber Minimum: CPU 2.4GHz, 1GB RAM. Recommended: 2, 4, 6, or 8-core CPU, based on need. 4GB RAM or higher. More CPU cores allow more workflows to run simultaneously. Software Requirements Windows Components Windows Message Queuing component: This component must be manually installed on the machine(s) hosting the Workflow Server and Subscriber. Microsoft .NET Framework 4.8: This component is installed as part of the Workflow installation process if not already installed. Windows Components Necessary for the Workflow Web Service IIS (Internet Information Services): This component must be manually installed on the computer hosting the Workflow Web Service. Note: You must install IIS 7 or higher. IIS Basic Authentication and Windows Authentication: This component must be manually installed on the computer hosting the Workflow Web Service. ASP.NET: This component must be manually installed on the computer hosting the Workflow Web Service. Note: If you are running Windows 8 or Windows Server 2012, you must install the ASP.NET 4.5 feature in IIS. On Windows 10 or Windows Server 2016, the feature may be listed as ASP.NET 4.6 or 4.7. Windows Components for Workflow Web IIS 7 or higher (Internet Information Services): This component must be manually installed on the computer hosting Laserfiche Workflow Web. IIS Windows Authentication: This component must be manually installed on the computer hosting the Workflow Web. ASP.NET 4.5, 4.6, or 4.7: This component must be manually installed on the computer hosting Workflow Web. Web Browser: Internet Explorer 9 or later (Internet Explorer 10 or later recommended), Firefox 24, Chrome 30, Safari Supported Database Management Systems SQL Server 2008 SQL Server 2008 R2 SQL Server 2008 Express SQL Server 2012 SQL Server 2012 Express SQL Server 2014 SQL Server 2014 Express SQL Server 2016 SQL Server 2017 SQL Server 2019 Oracle 10g (10.2.0.4+) Oracle 11g (11.1.0.7+) Oracle 11g R2 (11.2.0.1+) and higher Oracle 12c Note: Express editions of the SQL Server versions listed above are also supprted but not recommended for high-volume Workflow installations. Note: If you are using an Oracle DBMS, the Oracle Client 11.2.0.2.1 with the corresponding version of Oracle Data Access Components (ODAC) must be installed on the machine hosting the Workflow Server. Note: If you are using Oracle 12c, please install the 11.2.0.3 Oracle Client on the machine hosting the Workflow Server. Laserfiche Components Laserfiche Server 8.0 or higher (Laserfiche Server 10.4.1 or higher is recommended.) Note: To use all aspects of the business process feature, you must have Laserfiche Server 9.0 or higher and the Laserfiche Client 9.0 and/or Web Access 9.0 or higher and/or Web Client. Known Issues Component Known Issue Workflow Security If a workflow on a server version less than 10.4.2 tries to invoke a workflow on a server version 10.4.2 with Advanced Security activated, the workflow will fail. If you have a lower version server invoking a workflow on a 10.4.2 server, do not turn on advanced security until both servers are upgraded to 10.4.2. (206855) Workflow Installation If you upgrade from Workflow 8.3.2 to Workflow 9.0.x and then immediately upgrade to Workflow 9.1 or later without first using the Workflow 9.0.x configuration manager to configure Workflow, the Workflow 9.1 or later upgrade may not retain your Workflow Server settings from Workflow 8.3.2. You can upgrade from Workflow 8.3.2 to Workflow 9.1 or later. You do not need to do an intermediate upgrade to Workflow 9.0.x. (110343) Workflow Installation If you installed your Workflow Server and Subscriber on different computers, additional Microsoft Message Queuing configuration may be required. See the Workflow 9.1 Installation Guide for specific instructions. (78523) Workflow Installation If you installed your Workflow Server and Subscriber on different computers, you may need to provide domain authentication instead of using Local System Account when configuring the Workflow Subscriber. (77933) Workflow Installation When using the Repair option to restore as

rows.title: Release Notes for WebLink 8.2.1.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Sign in Release Notes for WebLink 8.2.1. Product: WebLink 8.2.1 KB: 1012940 Published: April 2, 2012 Last Updated: October 4, 2012 Summary Laserfiche WebLink 8.2.1 is a maintenance release that features performance enhancements and miscellaneous updates. For a more detailed listing of changes, please see Knowledge Base article 1012939. WebLink Server System Requirements Minimum requirements CPU: 2 GHz or faster processor Memory: 1 GB RAM Operating system: Windows XP (Service Pack 2) with IIS 5, Windows Server 2003 (Service Pack 1) with IIS 6, Windows Vista with IIS 7, Windows Server 2008 with IIS 7, Windows 7 with IIS 7.5, Windows Server 2008 R2 with IIS 7.5 Recommended Requirements CPU: 2.8 GHz or faster processor Memory: 2 GB RAM Server operating system: Windows Server 2003 with IIS 6, Windows Server 2008 with IIS 7, Windows Server 2008 R2 with IIS 7.5 Note: Hardware requirements may fluctuate based on the number of users logged in to the server. If you expect to have many simultaneous connections to your Web Access server, we encourage you to configure it with a faster CPU and/or add more RAM. If you are running IIS 7, make sure the IIS Metabase and IIS 6 configuration compatibility (under IIS 6 Management Compatibility) feature is enabled for your IIS 7 installation. If you are using Windows Server 2008 R2, you must enable the .NET Framework 3.5.1. Features option from the Add Features Wizard in the Server Manager prior to installing WebLink. Note: Internet Explorer 6 and 7 users should install Microsoft security update 947864 (MS08-024). Mobile Device: WebLink has been optimized for the iPhone, Android, Blackberry Storm, Blackberry Curve, and Blackberry Torch. However it may be functional on other mobile browsers. General Information If you are using Windows Server 2008 R2, you must enable the .NET Framework 3.5.1 Features option from the Add Features Wizard in the Server Manager prior to installing WebLink. Laserfiche WebLink works with Microsoft's Internet Information Services (IIS) to provide access to the documents in the Laserfiche repository. WebLink requires Internet Information Services 5.1 or above. This section describes some of the additional configuration that may be necessary to run WebLink on IIS 6 or IIS 7. A default installation of IIS 6.0 only serves static content. Ensure IIS 6.0 is configured to allow ASP.NET content. The IIS metabase stores configuration information for your installation of IIS. Some settings can't be edited using the IIS manager MMC snap-in, however, IIS includes a feature that allows you to directly edit the metabase XML while IIS is running. Ensure you enabled this feature. Ensure the following features are installed for Windows Vista (IIS 7) IIS WMI compatibility IIS Metabase and IIS 6 configuration compatibility IIS Scripting Tools IIS Management Service ASP.NET Windows Authentication WebLink Reporting is an analytics package available in the 32-bit version of WebLink 8.2. It tracks basic usage statistics such as number of sessions and number of searches, and can generate charts based on these statistics. The WebLink Reporting site is only accessible from the computer hosting WebLink. By default, the WebLink 8.2 document viewer will not display the folder breadcrumb above the image display. You can use the Show Breadcrumb option on the Document Viewer tab in the WebLink Designer to enable the control. WebLink 8.0.1 introduced language packs support. During the WebLink installation, select the Custom setup type to choose to manually install available Language Packs. By default, if you choose to display the installation in a non-English language, the installation attempts to automatically install the corresponding Language Pack. When logging in through WebLink, you must specify a user account configured for read-only access for WebLink to properly use retrieval licenses. Similarly, the Public Portal Licensing and Web Distribution Portal Licensing features only take effect when logged in with a user account configured for read-only access. Installation Information WebLink is available in both 32-bit and 64-bit. By default, the installation process will automatically install the version corresponding to your version of Microsoft Windows. Because IIS 6.0 does not support running 32-bit and 64-bit worker processes concurrently, you may need to explicitly install the 32-bit version of WebLink on a 64-bit version of Windows Server 2003 or Windows XP. rows.title: Users may see an "Unspecified error" message when storing documents from Quick Fields to a Laserfiche Cloud Repository
rows.detailTitle: Users may see an "Unspecified error" message when storing documents from Quick Fields to a Laserfiche Cloud Repository
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith Users may see an "Unspecified error" message when storing documents from Quick Fields to a Laserfiche Cloud Repository Product: Quick Fields KB: 1014519 Published: March 22, 2024 Last Updated: March 22, 2024 Summary Users may see an "Unspecified error" message when storing documents from Quick Fields to a Laserfiche Cloud Repository Possible Causes This issue may occur if the document class is configured to use the "Network repository" method to connect to the Laserfiche Cloud repository, without also checking the box to "Use TLS Connection" Resolution Go into the Document Class Options, modify the repository connection information, and check the box enabling the "Use TLS Connection" option Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

Take screenshots of all tabs of the workflow or ... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1014445/remote-agent-technical-support-ch ...
rows.title: Remote Agent Technical Support Checklist
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Sign in Remote Agent Technical Support Checklist Product: Remote Agents KB: 1014445 Published: April 13, 2023 Last Updated: September 11, 2025 Summary Certain files and pieces of information are frequently requested for support cases dealing with Remote Agent. Having them ready can expedite the handling of your support case. Commonly requested files and information General Technical Support Checklist Please provide the information listed in the General Technical Support Checklist. Remote Agent Details: Obtain the details about the Remote Agent. Take screenshots of all tabs of the Remote Agents details, including Queues, Workers, and History. Workflow or Business Process Instance Details: If the remote agent issue is related to the running of a workflow or business process instance, take note of the specific instance details, the exact time an error occurred, and the specific activity that the error occurred with. Take screenshots of all tabs of the workflow or business process instance details. Remote Agent Worker Logs: Acquire a copy of the Remote Agent Worker folder located under the BPMServer folder. By default, the path will be: C:\ProgramData\Laserfiche\BPMServer\. If possible, compress this folder in ZIP format to reduce its size. Windows Event Viewer Logs: Export the Application, Laserfiche, ProcessAutomation, and LocalAgent event viewer logs from the Remote Agent server. 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark
